hook.text: Stack bowling pins in rows: 1, then 2, then 3, then 4. The totals are 1, 3, 6, 10.

buildup.text: These are triangular numbers—each is the sum of consecutive integers starting from 1.

discovery.text: The formula is n(n+1)/2. The 100th triangular number is 5,050—the sum Gauss found as a child.

twist.text: Every positive integer is the sum of at most three triangular numbers. Gauss proved this too.

climax.text: Triangular numbers appear in handshake problems, Pascal's Triangle, and network connections.

punchline.text: Stack objects in a triangle and a beautiful formula emerges.
